Description: A popular dining spot in Masbate City serving authentic Masbate dishes and affordable comfort food favored by locals.

Name: Stairway to Heaven (Lablabig Hill)
Description: An iconic 140-step viewpoint in Barangay Balaoi offering breathtaking panoramic vistas of Dos Hermanos Islands, distant windmills, coastal villages, and the sparkling Pacific Ocean, particularly enchanting during sunrise and sunset when the sky transforms into magnificent colors.

Name: Macagutogot Cove and Rock Formation
Description: A hidden gem featuring dramatic rocky outcroppings and pristine beach stretch with crystal-clear turquoise waters perfect for swimming and snorkeling in a secluded setting, accessible by boat from Brgy. Pasaleng with boating services costing around 700 PHP for 3 persons.

Name: Kabigan Falls
Description: A spectacular 87-foot hidden waterfall plunging into a concave basin surrounded by dense jungle, accessible via a rewarding 30-minute trek from Bantay Abot Cave with natural pools at the base ideal for swimming.

Name: La Milagrosa Cove and Chapel
Location: Barangay La Virgen, Milagrosa, Badoc, Ilocos Norte
Description: A historic site commemorating the Virgin Mary statue arrival with a 2013-built chapel featuring unique maritime-inspired architecture designed like an upturned boat with bamboo fittings, offering both spiritual contemplation and scenic coastal views.

Name: Madongan Dam
Location: Barangay San Marcelino, Dingras, Ilocos Norte
Description: A majestic cascading water structure formed by Padsan River creating a stunning curtain-like waterfall effect, particularly impressive during rainy season when water volume increases dramatically, offering excellent opportunities for swimming and nature photography.

Name: Aglipay Caves and Campsite
Description: A series of 37-38 interconnecting limestone caverns with seven developed chambers featuring impressive stalactites, stalagmites, and flowstone formations, surrounded by rolling hills and dense forest perfect for camping and adventure activities.

Name: Claredita Falls (Victoria Falls)
Description: A spectacular five-tiered waterfall cascading down nearly vertical rocky mountain walls with tremendous force, dropping into beautiful blue lagoons at the pedestal where visitors can safely swim, accessible via 2 hours from Cabarroguis by 4x4 vehicle.

Name: Maddela Waterfalls
Description: A magnificent seven-staged cascade system with crystal-clear pools at each level perfect for swimming and recreation, offering short trekking times and forest resort facilities ideal for family nature retreats and romantic getaways.

Name: Governor's Rapids
Location: Barangay Bisangal, Divisoria Sur, Maddela Quirino
Description: A dramatic river site with limestone walls jutting perpendicular from the Cagayan River creating stunning geological formations framing swift deep blue turbulent waters, offering thrilling opportunities for kayaking and white-water rafting.

Name: Callao Cave
Location: Barangays Magdalo and Quibal, Peńablanca, Cagayan
Description: A seven-chambered limestone cavern famous for housing Callao Man (Homo Luzonensis) with 67,000-year-old human remains, featuring a cathedral-like chapel chamber with stunning natural light and impressive stalactites and stalagmites accessible via 184 concrete steps.

Name: Palaui Island
Description: A protected marine sanctuary featuring pristine powdery white beaches, crystal-clear turquoise waters ideal for snorkeling, and Cape EngaĂ±o Lighthouse built in 1890 offering panoramic views, famously featured as the filming location for US Survivor Season 27.

Name: Calayan Island
Description: A remote paradise featuring pristine white sand beaches, turquoise crystal-clear waters, and picturesque natural rock formations creating postcard-perfect scenery with multiple hidden coves ideal for snorkeling and sunset viewing.

Name: Anguib Beach
Description: Consistently rated as one of the finest beaches in the Philippines, featuring long stretches of powdery white sand meeting crystalline turquoise waters framed by captivating natural rock formations creating scenic viewpoints and protected swimming areas.

Name: Lussok Crystal Cave
Description: From its somber beginnings as a refuge for Japanese soldiers and native insurgents, Lussok Cave has metamorphosed into one of Luna's most well-loved attractions.

Name: San Antonio Island (Capones Island)
Description: A picturesque island shaped like a man's face with white sand beaches and green-tinted clear waters, famous for the historic Capones Island Lighthouse built in 1890 during Spanish colonial era with dramatic natural rock formations.

Name: Balite Falls
Description: A privately-owned waterfall resort in Cavite's Coffee Capital featuring crystal-clear mountain waters with multiple natural lagoons for relaxation and safe waterfall jumping areas, located just 1.5 hours from Manila via Cavitex highway.

Name: Gabaldon Falls
Description: A hidden gem waterfall with a 10-foot cascade surrounded by lush forests in the foothills of Sierra Madre, featuring rippling ice-cold mountain waters perfect for refreshing swims and natural bathing.

Name: Mount 387 (Batong Amat)
Description: An accessible mountain peak offering a rewarding 2-3 hour climb with rolling hills and panoramic scenic landscapes, with the summit providing stunning views extending across the Caraballo Mountain Range.

Name: Minalungao National Park
Description: A paradise for nature lovers featuring towering limestone formations surrounding an emerald-green PeĂ±aranda River, offering diverse adventure activities including bamboo rafting, river trekking, zip-lining, and a famous 1,000-step staircase.

Name: Pila Historic Town Center
Location: Barangay Santa Clara Norte, Pila, Laguna
Description: A National Historical Landmark designated in 2000 preserving exceptional examples of Spanish and American-era architecture with cobblestone streets and well-preserved ancestral homes dating back to the 17th-19th centuries reflecting colonial-period heritage.

Name: Lucban (Pahiyas Festival Town)
Description: Famous as the birthplace of the spectacular Pahiyas Festival celebrated annually on May 15 to honor San Isidro Labrador with approximately 600 houses participating in elaborate decorations using fresh fruits, vegetables, and colorful kipings competing for cash prizes.

Name: Villa Escudero Plantations and Resort
Description: A unique coconut plantation and resort famous for its signature waterfall restaurant where guests dine beside flowing waterfall with their feet submerged in refreshing running water, featuring a museum showcasing Filipino culture and history.

Name: Anilao Diving Site
Description: A premier diving and snorkeling destination with rich marine biodiversity featuring vibrant coral reefs, abundant tropical fish species, and underwater caves suitable for both certified advanced divers and beginner divers.

Name: Pagsanjan Falls
Description: An iconic waterfall accessed via scenic boat ride through winding river gorge, where visitors ride traditional bamboo rafts directly through the main cascade creating a thrilling and memorable encounter with water flowing over riders.

Name: Taal Heritage Town
Description: A town featuring cobblestone streets flanked by centuries-old Spanish colonial architecture preserved in ancestral homes, anchored by the grand Basilica of St. Martin of Tours showcasing traditional construction techniques and design from the colonial era.

Name: Polillo Islands
Description: A lesser-known island group offering pristine beaches, crystal-clear waters, and authentic island hopping opportunities in natural undeveloped settings away from mainstream tourist commercialization.

Name: Sumaguing Cave
Description: The "Big Cave" of Sagada, famous for its massive chambers, stunning limestone formations, and challenging spelunking routes. river passages, famous for hanging coffins in Echo Valley representing ancient Igorot burial practices and cultural heritage.

Name: Batad Rice Terraces
Description: Ancient rice terraces carved into mountainsides 2,000 years ago by Ifugao people in distinctive amphitheater shapes, still actively cultivated demonstrating sustainable farming practices perfected over centuries with excellent viewing platforms and cultural museums.

Name: Banaue Rice Terraces
Description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring expansive 2,000-year-old rice terraces carved into mountainsides representing one of humanity's greatest engineering achievements with viewing platforms, museums, and interpretive centers attracting over 100,000 visitors annually.

Name: Tappiyah Falls
Description: A stunning waterfall located near Batad Rice Terraces accessible via trek through Ifugao villages and traditional agricultural landscape, offering refreshing natural pool for swimming surrounded by cultural immersion experiences.

Name: Hanging Coffins of Sagada (Echo Valley)
Description: Ancient wooden coffins suspended from cliff faces in Echo Valley showcasing traditional burial practices of indigenous Igorot people and their cultural beliefs about the afterlife, accessible via hiking trail with over 100 positive reviews on TripAdvisor.

Name: Baguio Night Market
Description: A vibrant evening marketplace featuring local mountain produce, handicrafts, street food, and local artisan goods in open-air shopping environment showcasing authentic Baguio culture and local traditions.

Name: Arroceros Urban Forest Park
Description: Called the last lung of Manila, this 2-hectare urban forest park features over 60 tree species and numerous bird species with peaceful walking trails providing nature connection and respite from urban bustle.

Name: Manila Hidden Garden
Description: A transformed neglected space rejuvenated by local volunteers into a tranquil oasis featuring vibrant flora, peaceful walking paths, and green furniture created from recycled materials showcasing community activism and grassroots conservation.

Name: Paco Park
Description: A historic cemetery converted into a peaceful urban park featuring beautiful landscaping, walking paths, and graves of notable Filipino historical figures providing serene setting amid urban surroundings.

Name: Tangadan Falls
Location: Barangay Amontoc, San Gabriel, La Union
Description: A hidden gem featuring a picturesque two-tiered waterfall with cascading upper tier and impressive 40-foot main cascade framed by limestone cliffs and giant boulders, accessible via 30-minute trek through lush vegetation.

Name: Callao Cave Eco-Tourism Zone
Description: A 7-chambered limestone cave system featuring the discovery of Callao Man with 67,000-year-old human remains, accessible via 184 steps with impressive stalactites and stalagmites creating cathedral-like chambers with natural illumination.

Name: Mount Pinatubo Crater Lake
Description: A spectacular turquoise crater lake formed after Mt. Pinatubo's 1991 eruption, accessible via 4x4 vehicle and trekking offering surreal lunar-like landscape with stunning views of the volcanic crater.

Name: Pantihan Falls
Description: Also known as Balayungan Falls, this hidden gem features a series of small cascades and a large swimming area surrounded by lush greenery and boulders.

Name: Malibik-libik Falls
Description: A unique waterfall situated in a deep valley, known for its dramatic moss-covered cliff walls and a large, deep pool formed at the intersection of three rivers.

Name: Cabag Cave
Description: Also known as Lucsuhin Natural Bridge, this is the first large natural bridge reported in the Philippines, featuring a 75-meter tunnel-like opening carved by the Ylang-Ylang River.

Name: Mt. Marami
Description: Known for its unique "Silyang Bato" (Stone Chair) rock formation at the summit, offering a breathtaking 360-degree view of the Maragondon mountains and Corregidor Island.

Name: Mt. Nagpatong
Description: A historical hill featuring unique rock stacking formations and panoramic views; it is where the execution of Andres Bonifacio is historically memorialized.

Name: Mt. Buntis
Description: A verdant hill in Maragondon often hiked alongside Mt. Nagpatong, offering scenic trails through dense forests and significant historical links to the Philippine Revolution.

Name: Kaynipa Cove
Description: A secluded gray-sand beach cove accessible only by boat or a trek through the jungle, offering tranquil waters and a stunning view of the Bataan mountain range.

Name: Paniman Beach
Description: A peaceful beach located near the Puerto Azul resort area, famous for its calm waters, soft gray sand, and a lush rainforest backdrop frequently visited by wild monkeys.

Name: Patungan Beach Cove
Description: An underrated coastal gem featuring fine sand and clear waters, popular for island hopping to nearby rock formations and a relaxed, local fishing village atmosphere.

Name: Hidden Tapsihan
Description: A legendary "hole-in-the-wall" eatery in Kawit tucked away in a narrow alley, famous for its sweet-salty beef tapa that has remained a local favorite for decades.

Name: Alitaptap Art Cafe
Description: A rustic, art-filled cafe located within a private farm, offering organic coffee and a creative atmosphere where local artists often gather for exhibitions and jam sessions.

Name: Buhay Forest
Description: An eco-tourism site featuring a network of hiking trails, viewing decks, and unique tree houses, promoting forest conservation and local biodiversity.

Name: Ligpo Island
Description: A small rocky island in Balayan Bay known for its exceptional diving spots, featuring underwater caves, vibrant coral gardens, and diverse marine life.

Name: Layong Bilog Falls
Description: Also known as Layong Bilog River, this scenic spot features clear waters and unique rock formations that create natural pools perfect for river tubing and riverside camping.

Name: Mt. Banoy
Description: The highest peak in Batangas City, hosting several communication towers and offering an unparalleled view of Batangas Bay, Mindoro, and the surrounding mountains.

Name: Quitinday Green Hills
Description: Often called the "Chocolate Hills of Bicol," this site features hundreds of rolling green mounds that offer a spectacular panoramic view of Mount Mayon.

Name: Kawa-Kawa Hill
Description: Unique for being a "hill without a peak," its crater-like summit resembles a cauldron (kawa) and features life-size Stations of the Cross and a 360-degree view of Ligao.

Name: Subic White Beach
Description: Famous for its unique pinkish-white sand caused by crushed red organ pipe corals, this beach on Calintaan Island offers crystal-clear turquoise waters.

Name: Paguriran Island
Description: A rocky islet featuring a hidden lagoon in its center, accessible by wading through knee-deep water during low tide, offering a surreal and protected swimming experience.

Name: Sumlang Lake
Description: A serene 9-hectare lake offering bamboo raft tours with the most iconic and unobstructed reflection view of the perfectly symmetrical Mayon Volcano.

Name: Busay Falls
Description: A magnificent seven-tiered waterfall cascading from a total height of 91 meters, with the lowest tier featuring a wide pool ideal for swimming and family picnics.

Name: Hoyop-Hoyopan Cave
Description: A multi-layered limestone cave named after the "hoyop" (blowing) sound of the wind; it historically served as a burial site and a shelter during the Japanese occupation.

Name: Binurong Point
Description: Often called the "Batanes of Catanduanes," this site features stunning sea-facing cliffs and rolling green hills that offer a dramatic 360-degree view of the Pacific Ocean.

Name: Malabsay Falls
Description: A breathtaking natural waterfall located on the slopes of Mt. Isarog. It features a 40-foot drop into a wide, crystal-clear, and very cold natural swimming pool surrounded by a dense rainforest.

Name: Calaruega, Philippines
Description: A serene sanctuary and retreat center famous for its "Transfiguration Chapel" perched on a hill. It is known for its beautiful brick architecture, hanging bridges, and panoramic mountain views.

Name: Katungkulan Beach Resort
Description: Known locally as "Boracay de Cavite," this is a small cove with fine white sand located inside a Marine Base. It offers a clean, calm, and relatively secluded beach experience near Metro Manila.

Name: Kaybiang Tunnel
Description: The longest underground road tunnel in the Philippines (300 meters), cutting through Mt. Pico de Loro. It is a popular pitstop for cyclists and riders due to its scenic views of the coastline.

Name: Sabtang Lighthouse
Description: A prominent stone lighthouse perched on a cliff at the entrance of Sabtang Port, offering panoramic views of the sea and the rugged Batanes landscape.

Name: Chavayan Village
Description: A beautifully preserved heritage village where traditional Ivatan stone houses with thick thatched cogon roofs line narrow, winding streets.

Name: Morong Beach
Description: Famous for the "Nakabuang Arch," a natural limestone rock formation on a white sand beach that has become an iconic symbol of Batanes.

Name: Valugan Boulder Beach
Description: A unique shoreline covered with smooth, massive boulders spewed by Mt. Iraya centuries ago, known for the dramatic sound of waves crashing against rocks.

Name: Mt. Iraya
Description: An active volcano and the highest point in Batanes, offering a challenging trek through mossy forests and rewarding views of the entire island.

Name: Tayid Lighthouse
Description: A red-lanterned lighthouse located on a hill overlooking the Pacific Ocean and the Diura Fishing Village, designed to mimic traditional Ivatan architecture.

Name: Dipnaysuhuan Japanese Tunnel
Description: This 250-metre tunnel, constructed from volcanic rocks and cement, served as a shelter and lookout for Japanese soldiers during World War II from 1941 to 1945.

Name: House of Dakay
Description: Recognized as the oldest stone house in Batanes (built in 1889), it has survived numerous earthquakes and remains a prime example of Ivatan masonry.

Name: Diura Fishing Village
Description: A coastal community known for the "Kapayvanuvanua" ritual, which signals the start of the summer fishing season for "Arayu" (Mahi-mahi).

Name: Buntun Bridge
Description: The second longest bridge in the Philippines, spanning the mighty Cagayan River and offering a spectacular sunset view.

Name: Iguig Calvary Hills
Description: An 11-hectare rolling hill featuring life-size Stations of the Cross and a century-old church overlooking the Cagayan River.

Name: Our Lady of Piat Basilica
Description: A major pilgrimage site housing the 16th-century Black Virgin Mary image, known as the Mother of Cagayan.

Name: Magapit Suspension Bridge
Description: Often called the "Golden Gate of the Philippines," this suspension bridge is a vital link across the Cagayan River with a unique architectural design.

Name: Cape Engańo Lighthouse
Description: A 19th-century Spanish lighthouse perched on the northernmost tip of Palaui Island, offering a 360-degree view of the Pacific and Babuyan Islands.

Name: Crocodile Island
Description: A rocky outcrop near Palaui Island that resembles a floating crocodile, popular for its vibrant coral reefs and snorkeling spots.

Name: Punta Verde
Description: The main gateway and campsite for Palaui Island, featuring a tranquil shoreline and serving as the jump-off point for forest treks.

Name: San Jacinto Ermita Church
Description: An elevated 18th-century chapel with a distinct facade, originally used as a fortress and a lookout point during the Spanish era.

Name: Camalaniugan Bell
Description: Houses the oldest church bell in the Philippines (San Jacinto Bell), forged in 1595, located in a quiet town along the riverbank.

Name: St. Dominic de Guzman Parish Church - Centro, Lal-lo, Cagayan (Archdiocese of Tuguegarao)
Description: Served as the original seat of the Diocese of Nueva Segovia, established in 1595. It was the first cathedral in Northern Luzon before the seat was transferred to Vigan in 1758 due to flooding.

Name: World's Largest Butaka
Description: A Guinness World Record holder, this massive wooden arm chair (Butaka) symbolizes the town's pride in its local furniture industry.

Name: Magat Dam
Description: One of the largest dams in Asia, offering a vast reservoir for boating, fishing, and a scenic park for family gatherings.

Name: Bonsai Forest
Description: A unique 20,000-hectare natural forest of stunted trees along the Pacific coast, creating a surreal and miniature landscape.

Name: Sierra Madre National Park
Description: The largest protected area in the Philippines, housing a vast wilderness of rainforests and endemic species along the coast.

Name: Dibulo Falls
Description: A towering 100-meter waterfall hidden deep in the forest, featuring a massive wall of cascading water and a cool natural pool.

Name: Fuyot Springs National Park
Description: A wildlife sanctuary and eco-tourism park offering caves, natural springs, and hiking trails within a protected forest area.

Name: Palanan Blue Lagoon
Description: A breathtaking freshwater lagoon with crystal-clear turquoise waters surrounded by a dense, untouched tropical forest.

Name: Dicotcotan Beach
Description: A secluded white sand beach on the Pacific side of Isabela, offering pristine waters and a view of the Sierra Madre mountains.

Name: Our Lady of the Pillar Cathedral
Description: A modern yet historic cathedral in the heart of Cauayan City, known for its elegant interior and religious significance.

Name: Camp Samal
Description: A historic military camp and training ground used during the Japanese occupation, now a serene park with monument markers.

Name: Queen Isabela Park
Description: A large, well-maintained provincial park featuring monuments, gardens, and fountains, serving as the cultural hub of Isabela.

Name: St. Dominic Cathedral
Description: An 18th-century Baroque church and the seat of the Diocese of Bayombong, featuring a unique octagonal bell tower.

Name: Capisaan Cave
Description: The fifth longest cave system in the Philippines, boasting rare calcite formations and an underground river system.

Name: Lower Magat Eco-Tourism Park
Description: A 24,000-hectare forest reserve offering a lagoon for boating, camping sites, and a variety of outdoor activities.

Name: Balete Pass (Dalton Pass)
Description: A high mountain pass that was the site of a major World War II battle, now marked by shrines and viewpoints.

Name: Imugan Falls
Description: A two-tiered waterfall tucked in a lush valley, known for its refreshing cold waters and scenic forest trails.

Name: Dupax del Sur Church
Description: San Vicente Ferrer Parish, a National Cultural Treasure known for its preserved brick architecture and wooden altarpiece.

Name: Mt. Ugo
Description: A popular hiking destination shared with Benguet, featuring vast pine forests and sweeping views of the Cordilleras.

Name: Lintungan Falls
Description: A multi-tiered hidden waterfall in Quezon town, offering a secluded spot for nature lovers and swimming.

Name: Ambaguio Sky Escape 360
Description: Known as the "Little Baguio" of Nueva Vizcaya, offering cool temperatures and a view of the clouds over the mountains.

Name: Siitan River
Description: A pristine river known for its turquoise waters and the Siitan Nature Park, perfect for river cruising and tubing.

Name: Bimmapor Rock Formation
Description: A massive rock formation along the river that resembles a sinking ship (Bapor), popular for rock climbing and photography.

Name: Diamond Cave
Description: A stunning cave system featuring shimmering white calcite formations that resemble diamonds when hit by light.

Name: Susong Dalaga Cave
Description: One of Cabarroguis' most popular natural tourist attractions is Susong Dalaga, twin conical mountains that resemble a young woman's breast.

Name: Ganano Falls
Description: A majestic waterfall with a massive volume of water, located within the Baguio Village eco-tourism area of Diffun.

Name: Pensal Falls
Description: A multi-tiered hidden gem in Maddela, known for its deep green pools and peaceful forest atmosphere.

Name: Bangui Wind Farm
Description: The first power-generating wind farm in Southeast Asia, featuring 20 massive wind turbines lined up along the scenic Bangui Bay.

Name: Cape Bojeador Lighthouse
Description: A cultural heritage structure built in 1892 that still guides ships entering the Philippine archipelago from the north.

Name: Malacańang of the North
Description: A grand presidential mansion overlooking Paoay Lake, serving as a museum that showcases the history and lifestyle of the Marcos family.

Name: Paoay Sand Dunes
Description: A 88-square-kilometer desert-like expanse offering adrenaline-pumping 4x4 rides and sandboarding activities along the coast.

Name: La Paz Sand Dunes
Description: A protected sandy coastal desert near Laoag City, famous as a filming location for local and international movies like "Mad Max."

Name: Patapat Viaduct
Description: An elevated coastal bridge winding along the foot of the mountains, offering a breathtaking view of the Pasaleng Bay.

Name: Sinking Bell Tower
Description: A massive 45-meter bell tower known for sinking into the ground at a rate of an inch per year due to its heavy weight and sandy foundation.

Name: Museo nina Juan at Antonio Luna
Description: The restored ancestral home of the world-renowned Filipino painter Juan Luna, housing replicas of his famous artworks.

Name: La Virgen Milagrosa
Description: A 17th-century brick church that is home to the miraculous image of the Virgin of Milagrosa, the patroness of Ilocos Norte.

Name: Dos Hermanos Islands
Description: Two identical rocky islets near Blue Lagoon that are accessible by foot during low tide, offering a great spot for photography.

Name: Dingras Church
Description: The hauntingly beautiful roofless ruins of an Augustinian church that was once one of the largest in the Philippines.

Name: Museo Ilocos Norte
Description: Housed in an old tobacco warehouse, this museum features a wide collection of Ilocano cultural artifacts and traditional house models.

Name: Vea Eco Farm
Description: A scenic agricultural spot at the foot of the mountains, popular for its vibrant flower gardens and cool mountain breeze.

Name: Culili Point Sand Dunes
Description: A rugged coastal area where the sand dunes meet the sea, offering dramatic landscapes and a secluded atmosphere.

Name: Bantay Watch Tower
Description: Built in 1591, this belfry served as a watchtower against pirates and offers a panoramic view of Vigan and the mountains

Name: Baluarte Resort and Zoo
Description: An interactive wildlife sanctuary owned by Chavit Singson, featuring a safari gallery and various exotic animals.

Name: Crisologo Museum
Description: The former residence of the Crisologo clan, now a museum preserving political memorabilia and traditional Vigan furniture.

Name: Hidden Garden Vigan
Description: A lush tropical sanctuary and plant nursery that features a restaurant serving authentic Ilocano dishes in a garden setting.

Name: Padre Burgos House
Description: The birthplace of Jose Burgos, one of the three GOMBURZA martyrs, now a branch of the National Museum.

Name: Plaza Salcedo Dancing Fountain
Description: A historic plaza that features a nightly laser and musical fountain show, showcasing the history of Vigan.

Name: Quirino Bridge
Description: Also known as Banaoang Bridge, this iconic orange arch bridge spans the Abra River and offers a spectacular mountain view.

Name: Pinsal Falls
Description: Known as the largest waterfall in Ilocos, featuring several natural pools and a legendary giant footprint on the rock.

Name: Minor Basilica and Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of the Assumption
Description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site perched on a hill, accessible via a grand stone stairway, serving as a fortress-church.

Name: Syquia Mansion Museum
Description: The ancestral home of Dona Alicia Syquia, wife of President Elpidio Quirino, showcasing opulent 19th-century interiors.

Name: Mindoro Beach
Description: A quiet beach area in Vigan known for its dramatic sunsets and a long stretch of dark, fine sand.

Name: Arce Mansion
Description: A heritage house where visitors can experience living like a "Vigan Ilustrado" by wearing traditional Filipiniana costumes.

Name: Skyline View Deck
Description: A mountain-side viewing deck that offers a sea of clouds experience and a 360-degree view of the Ilocos mountains.

Name: Caniaw Heritage Park
Description: A forest park and reforestation project that offers nature trails and a glimpse of the local flora and fauna.

Name: Urbiztondo Beach
Description: The heart of the La Union surfing scene, known for its consistent waves, trendy cafes, and vibrant nightlife.

Name: Ma-Cho Temple
Description: A majestic Taoist temple built in 1977, perched on a hill overlooking San Fernando Bay, dedicated to the sea goddess Ma-Cho.

Name: Gapuz Grapes Farm
Description: A unique agricultural attraction where visitors can experience grape picking and taste locally produced grape products.

Name: Nalvo Pebble Beach
Description: A unique coastline covered entirely in smooth pebbles and stones instead of sand, popular for stone-picking and photography.

Name: Bahay na Bato
Description: A rest house and museum built from pebbles and stones found on the beach, featuring intricate wood and stone sculptures.

Name: St. Catherine of Alexandria Parish - The Shrine of Our Lady of Namacpacan
Description: St. Catherine of Alexandria Parish, home to the miraculous 6-foot tall image of Our Lady of Namacpacan.

Name: Baluarte Watchtower
Description: A 400-year-old restored Spanish watchtower that once warned the locals of impending pirate attacks from the sea.

Name: Immuki Island
Description: A series of lagoons and rocky islets with crystal-clear emerald waters, perfect for swimming and snorkeling.

Name: Occalong Falls
Description: A serene and multi-tiered waterfall located in a forest area, offering a refreshing and secluded freshwater dip.

Name: Poro Point Lighthouse
Description: A historic lighthouse located on a cliff, offering a stunning view of the Poro Point Freeport Zone and the West Philippine Sea.

Name: Basilica of Our Lady of Manaoag
Description: One of the most visited pilgrimage sites in the country, known for the miraculous image of the Virgin Mary.

Name: Bolinao Falls 1
Description: A beautiful waterfall with a wide turquoise pool, perfect for swimming and cliff jumping.

Name: Bolinao Falls 2
Description: Located near Falls 1, this tier offers a more tranquil atmosphere with scenic cascades and shaded areas.

Name: Patar Beach
Description: Known as the "Boracay of the North," featuring creamy golden sand and clear waters on the western tip of Pangasinan.

Name: Cape Bolinao Lighthouse
Description: The second tallest lighthouse in the Philippines, built in 1903 on a cliff overlooking the South China Sea.

Name: Enchanted Cave
Description: A subterranean cave featuring a deep, crystal-clear freshwater pool that is safe for swimming.

Name: Cabongaoan Beach
Description: A secluded beach famous for its "Death Pool," a natural tidal pool that creates dramatic splashes during high tide.

Name: Tambobong Beach
Description: A peaceful fishing village turned tourist spot, offering white sand and calm waters, serving as a jump-off to Colibra Island.

Name: Colibra Island
Description: A small, remote island with clear waters and fine sand, also known as Snake Island (though snakes are rarely seen now).

Name: Tayug Sunflower Eco Park
Description: A massive farm featuring thousands of sunflowers arranged in a maze, along with various other vibrant flower gardens.

Name: Mount Pulag National Park
Description: The highest peak in Luzon, famous for its "sea of clouds" and dwarf bamboo slopes, offering a spiritual and breathtaking hiking experience.

Name: Burnham Park
Description: The "mother of all parks" in Baguio, featuring a scenic lake for boating, flower gardens, and a dedicated biking area in the heart of the city.

Name: Mines View Park
Description: A popular overlook offering a panoramic view of the Amburayan Valley and the abandoned gold and copper mines of Itogon.

Name: Baguio Mansion House : The Presidential Museum
Description: The official summer residence of the President of the Philippines, known for its grand ornate gate and manicured lawns.

Name: Wright Park
Description: Located just outside The Mansion, it features a long "Pool of Pines" and is the city's primary area for recreational horseback riding.

Name: Camp John Hay
Description: A former US military rest and recreation facility turned eco-tourism zone, famous for its pine forests, golf course, and "Bell House."

Name: Tam-awan Village
Description: An artist's colony and reconstructed Cordillera village showcasing traditional Ifugao and Kalinga huts alongside local art galleries.

Name: La Trinidad Strawberry Farm
Description: An agricultural valley where visitors can experience picking fresh strawberries and taste unique strawberry-flavored local treats.

Name: Valley of Colors
Description: A massive community art installation consisting of colorful murals painted across dozens of houses on the hillside of Stobosa.

Name: Diplomat Hotel
Description: A hauntingly beautiful ruin of a former seminary and hotel on Dominican Hill, known for its history and panoramic views of the city.

Name: Good Shepherd Convent
Description: Famous for its charitable mission and high-quality local products, particularly its signature Ube Jam and Strawberry preserves.

Name: Bell Church
Description: A colorful Taoist temple complex featuring intricate Chinese architecture, dragon sculptures, and serene gardens near the Baguio border.

Name: Northern Blossom Flower Farm
Description: A hillside flower farm famous for its varieties of cabbage roses and other blooms, offering a stunning view of Mt. Pulag and Mt. Tabayoc.

Name: Mt. Yangbew
Description: Also known as "Little Mt. Pulag," it offers an easy trek to a wide grassy summit with 360-degree views and frequent horseback sightings

Name: Mt. Kalugong Cultural Park
Description: Named for its hat-shaped rock formation, this park features a limestone forest, a traditional village, and a cozy cliffside cafe.

Name: Lumiang Burial Cave
Description: A sacred site containing hundreds of stacked wooden coffins, some dating back over 500 years, reflecting ancient Igorot burial traditions.

Name: Bomod-ok Falls
Description: Also called the "Big Falls," it features a 200-foot drop into a wide, freezing cold pool, accessible via a trek through the Fidelisan Rice Terraces.

Name: Marlboro Hills
Description: A high-altitude ridge known for the "sea of clouds" at sunrise and a grassy plateau where wild horses are sometimes seen.

Name: Kiltepan Viewpoint
Description: The most famous sunrise spot in Sagada, offering a spectacular view of the clouds rolling over the Kiltepan rice terraces.

Name: Blue Soil Hills
Description: A unique geological site where the soil has a distinct bluish-green hue due to high copper sulfate content, surrounded by pine trees.

Name: Lake Danum
Description: A tranquil mountain lake surrounded by pine forests, serving as a popular spot for sunset viewing and lakeside picnics

Name: Hapao Rice Terraces
Description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its unique stone-walled terraces and the refreshing Bogyah Hot Spring at the end of the trail

Name: Mayoyao Rice Terraces
Description: More rugged and vast than other terraces, these stone-walled wonders represent the exceptional engineering of the Ifugao people.

Name: Bangaan Rice Terraces
Description: A picturesque and accessible terrace cluster that perfectly frames a traditional Ifugao village in the center of the valley.

Name: Nagacadan Rice Terraces
Description: A UNESCO site featuring a split-level terrace system that follows the natural contour of the mountains in the historic town of Kiangan.

Name: Kiangan War Memorial Shrine
Description: A monument shaped like an Ifugao hut commemorating the end of WWII in the Philippines, where General Yamashita surrendered.

Name: Chico River White Water Rafting
Description: An adrenaline-pumping experience along the "River of Life," featuring Class III to IV rapids amidst spectacular canyon views.

Name: Mini Palawan
Description: A spectacural canyon views of the winding Chico River.

Name: Balbalasang-Balbalan National Park
Description: Known as the "Green Heart of the Cordillera," it is one of the most biodiverse protected areas in the region with pristine pine forests.

Name: Mount Binuluan
Description: A majestic mountain known for its active fumaroles and hot springs, considered sacred by the local Kalinga tribes.

Name: Kaparkan Falls
Description: A stunning multi-tiered travertine waterfall that resembles a series of limestone pools cascading down the mountain.

Name: Piit Falls
Description: A secluded waterfall hidden in a deep ravine, featuring clear emerald waters and a peaceful, untouched environment.

Name: Lusuac Spring
Description: A crystal-clear natural spring and swimming hole surrounded by massive trees and limestone formations.

Name: Apao Rolling Hills
Description: A vast expanse of verdant hills that offer a landscape similar to Batanes, located in the remote highlands of Tineg.

Name: Don Mariano Marcos Bridge
Description: A long steel bridge that provides a scenic view of the Abra River and serves as a vital gateway to the province.

Name: Gabriela Silang Monument
Description: A shrine dedicated to the "Joan of Arc of Ilocandia," who was born in Abra and led the revolt against Spanish rule.

Name: Tangadan Tunnel
Description: A 40-meter man-made tunnel through the mountain that serves as the iconic welcome landmark for visitors entering Abra.

Name: Dupag Rock Formation
Description: A jagged cluster of towering limestone rocks and boulders that create a natural fortress-like landscape.

Name: Bayugao Falls
Description: A six-tiered waterfall system with multiple natural pools, perfect for swimming and surrounded by a dense forest.

Name: Maton River
Description: A clean and shallow river with large rock formations, popular among locals for picnics and refreshing river dips.

Name: Apayao River
Description: The province's primary waterway, known for its pristine waters, limestone cliffs, and scenic boat ride opportunities.

Name: Mount Kalawit
Description: A high peak in Mt. Province that offers a panoramic view of the Cordillera range and the surrounding river systems.

Name: Bontoc Museum
Description: Founded by Belgian sisters, it houses an extensive collection of traditional tools, clothing, and photos of Cordillera tribes.

Name: Maligcong Rice Terraces
Description: Famous for the stone-walled terraces and the view from Mt. Kupapey, which shows the terraces blanketed in morning fog.

Name: Mainit Hot Springs
Description: Natural hot springs located in the village of Mainit, known for their high temperature and therapeutic sulfuric waters.

Name: Gueday Stone Calendars
Description: Ancient rock formations used by ancestors to determine the agricultural cycle based on the alignment of the sun.

Name: Aguid Rice Terraces
Description: A beautiful set of terraces located near Bomod-ok Falls, showcasing the agricultural mastery of the northern Sagada people.

Name: Mt. Binuluan Fumaroles
Description: Active volcanic steam vents located on the slopes of Mt. Binuluan, creating a unique and rugged geothermal landscape.

Name: Abra River Viewpoint
Description: A high vantage point overlooking the winding Abra River as it cuts through the massive mountain canyons of Luba.

Name: Calm Springs
Description: A restaurant with springs in Penarrubia.

Name: Kimmul-Ong River
Description: A clean water river with bamboo boat activities.

Name: Sea of Clouds View Deck
Description: A high elevated location on which you can observe the dreamy sea of clouds.

Name: Vayang Rolling Hills
Description: A verdant expanse of hills providing a panoramic view of the West Philippine Sea and the majestic Mt. Iraya.

Name: Mt. Matarem
Description: An extinct volcano in Batan Island, offering a challenging hike with views of the entire island and surrounding sea.

Name: Ivana Port
Description: The main gateway to Sabtang Island, featuring a historic stone pier and crystal-clear turquoise waters.

Name: Rapang Cliffs
Description: Dramatic limestone cliffs on the eastern side of Itbayat Island, famous for the stone bell formation and grazing cattle.

Name: Torongan Cave
Description: An ancient cave that once served as a settlement for Austronesian people, featuring a large opening facing the sea.

Name: Spring of Youth (Rakuh-a-idekan)
Description: A natural cold spring pool carved into the side of a cliff, offering a refreshing swim with a view of Mt. Iraya.

Name: San Jose de Ivana Church
Description: A Spanish-era stone church built in 1787, known for its massive belfry and proximity to the famous Honesty Coffee Shop.

Name: St. Philomene Church
Description: Known as the widest church in the Philippines, this red-brick structure is a masterpiece of Spanish colonial architecture.

Name: Baggao Bluewater Falls and Cave
Description: A breathtaking nature complex where blue-hued waters flow through limestone caves and over rocky falls.

Name: Duba Cave
Description: A massive limestone cave system in Baggao featuring unique stalactite formations and underground streams.

Name: Fuga Island
Description: A secluded island in the Babuyan Channel known for its white sand beaches, coral reefs, and tranquil atmosphere.

Name: Dalupiri Island
Description: A remote island featuring rugged coastlines and a small community living amidst untouched natural beauty.

Name: Smith Volcano (Mt. Babuyan)
Description: A cinder cone volcano on Babuyan Island known for its perfect cone shape and occasional volcanic activity.

Name: Sentinela Beach
Description: A wild, untouched beach in Claveria known for its strong waves and views of the Camiguin Norte mountains.

Name: Portabaga Falls
Description: A multi-tiered waterfall located right along the highway, featuring clear waters and man-made swimming pools.

Name: Mapurao White Beach
Description: A pristine white sand beach in the northernmost tip of Cagayan, often used as a jump-off point for Palaui Island.

Name: Siwangag Cove
Description: Peaceful sandy & pebbled beach with calm, clear water, marine life & a relaxed feel.

Name: Engano Cove
Description: Picturesque cove with a beach & natural area offering elevated sea views & a historic lighthouse.

Name: Tangol Cove
Description: A breathtaking view in Palaqui Island overlooking Tangol Twins.

Name: Aparri Delta (Cagayan River Mouth)
Description: The point where the longest river in the Philippines, the Cagayan River, meets the Babuyan Channel.

Name: San Telmo Church
Description: A historic church in the port town of Aparri, serving as a beacon of faith for generations of sailors and fishermen.

Name: Tumauini Church (San Matias Parish)
Description: A 17th-century church made of red bricks, famous for its unique cylindrical bell tower and intricate clay carvings.

Name: Aparri Marker
Description: A marker of Aparri where history, culture, and the sea come together at the edge of Luzon.

Name: Navagan Floating Restaurant
Description: A floating restaurant stretching along Navagan River.

Name: Echague Church (San Pancracio Parish)
Description: A beautiful brick church in Isabela known for its elegant facade and historical significance in the region.

Name: Ilagan Sanctuary (Pinzal Falls)
Description: An eco-tourism park featuring Pinzal Falls, a mini-zoo, and various outdoor activities within a protected forest.

Name: Our Lady of Atocha Church
Description: A Spanish-era church made of clay and bricks, recognized for its distinct architectural style and heritage.

Name: Dibacong Whitesand Beach
Description: A powdery whitesand that has clear water and good for swimming and picnic.

Name: Dipudo Rock Formation and Beaches
Description: A rock formation in Dipudo with white sand beach.

Name: Honeymoon Island
Description: A secluded islet in Divilacan featuring pristine sand and clear waters, perfect for remote camping and exploration.

Name: San Mariano Crocodile Sanctuary
Description: A conservation area dedicated to protecting the rare Philippine Freshwater Crocodile in its natural habitat.

Name: St. Ferdinand Cathedral
Description: The seat of the Diocese of Ilagan, featuring a grand interior and serving as a major religious hub in the province.

Name: Bangan Hill National Park
Description: A historic hill in Bayombong that serves as a park, offering trails and a view of the town and mountains.

Name: Salinas Salt Springs
Description: A natural landmark where saline water once flowed, creating unique white mineral formations on the hillside.

Name: Mount Palali
Description: A mountain peak popular with hikers for its diverse flora and views of the Magat River valley.

Name: Villa Verde Trail
Description: A historic trail used during the war, now a popular route for hikers and off-road enthusiasts.

Name: Malico Viewpoint
Description: A viewpoint that gives travelers the chance to behold San Nicolas' picturesque landscape.

Name: Banila Water Falls
Description: A stunning waterfall hidden in Dupax Del Sur.

Name: Mapalyao Falls
Description: A scenic waterfall located in the town of Quezon, offering a peaceful and lush environment for visitors.

Name: Quirino Province Museum
Description: A modern museum showcasing the indigenous culture, history, and natural wonders of the province of Quirino.

Name: Landingan Viewpoint
Description: A colorful place in Nagtipunan. Situated on top of the hill, this area became prominent because of its vibrant and stunningly beautiful flowers.

Name: Gawagan Rock Formation
Description: A serene oasis in Quirino Province, where lush landscapes and diverse wildlife awaits discovery.

Name: Mactol Falls
Description: One of the most remote and majestic waterfalls in Quirino, located deep within the Sierra Madre range.

Name: Camalaniugan Hanging Bridge
Description: A long suspension bridge over the Cagayan River, providing a thrilling crossing and views of the river.

Name: Taggat Lagoon
Description: A serene coastal lagoon in Claveria surrounded by rocky cliffs and clear, calm waters.

Name: Gamu Church
Description: A historic brick church in Isabela dedicated to Saint Rose of Lima, featuring classical Spanish architecture.

Name: Banti Falls
Description: A small but charming waterfall located near the town of Bayombong, popular among local hikers.

Name: Maddela Ecotourism Park
Description: A park dedicated to the natural beauty of Maddela, featuring trails near the river and forest areas.

Name: Maddela Falls
Description: A captivating tourist attraction renowned for its stunning cascades and serene natural beauty.

Name: Pinahiw View Point
Description: A viewpoint in Quirino where you can see a sea of clouds or the sparkling Cagayan River depending on the time and weather.

Name: Mt. Tapulao
Description: The highest peak in the Zambales Mountains, famous for its "Pine Tree" trails and cold temperatures, offering a grueling but rewarding climb.

Name: Anawangin Cove
Description: A crescent-shaped cove famous for its volcanic ash "sand" and a lush forest of agoho trees that grew after the 1991 Pinatubo eruption.

Name: Nagsasa Cove
Description: A larger, quieter version of Anawangin with rolling hills and a pristine river, accessible via a boat ride or a trek across Mt. Nagsasa.

Name: Mt. Pundaquit
Description: A popular day-hike destination that serves as the gateway to Anawangin Cove, offering panoramic views of the Pundaquit coastline.

Name: Mt. Balingkilat
Description: Known as the "Mountain of Thunder," it is one of the highest peaks in the Cawag Quad-Peak series, offering a challenging assault and sea views.

Name: Mt. Cinco Picos
Description: A mountain featuring five distinct peaks that overlook Silanguin Cove, known for its steep trails and expansive grasslands.

Name: Mt. Dayungan
Description: Part of the Cawag mountain range, this peak is known for its technical trails and the stunning "sea of clouds" during the rainy season.

Name: Mt. Bira-bira
Description: A minor peak often traversed alongside Mt. Balingkilat, offering a scenic vantage point of the Zambales coastal coves.

Name: Silanguin Cove
Description: The most remote cove in San Antonio, featuring reddish sand and a very peaceful atmosphere, perfect for extreme off-grid camping.

Name: Talisayen Cove
Description: A hidden gem between Anawangin and Nagsasa, known for its calm waters and small, quiet community of local caretakers.

Name: Camara Island
Description: A small rocky islet near Capones Island featuring a sandbar that appears during low tide and unique geological formations.

Name: Potipot Island
Description: A tiny, "walkable" island with powdery white sand and clear waters, often called the "Little Boracay" of Zambales.

Name: Magalawa Island
Description: A 56-hectare island famous for its creamy white sand and a sanctuary for starfish, located just a short boat ride from the mainland.

Name: Hermana Menor Island
Description: A private island open to the public, featuring pristine white sand beaches and a very clear marine sanctuary for snorkeling.

Name: Mapanuepe Lake
Description: A "New Zealand-like" lake formed after the Pinatubo eruption, featuring a sunken church steeple and vast green pine-tree-lined banks.

Name: Mt Kapigpiglatan
Description: A scenic hill in San Felipe that offers an easy trek with views of the Maloma River and the West Philippine Sea.

Name: Bugtong Sunflower Farm
Description: A sunflower farm near the Capandanan Falls.

Name: Capandanan falls
Description: A waterfall with clear water and rock formation.

Name: Veto Falls
Description: A waterfall with cool temperature and clean water with hiking trail connected to Mt. Kapigpiglatan.

Name: Anghalo Falls
Description: A series of three small waterfalls with natural pools, considered a sacred spot by the local Aeta community.

Name: Lubong-Nangoloan Falls
Description: A hidden waterfall with a wide basin and massive rock formations, perfect for a refreshing dip after a mountain trek.

Name: Pamulaklakin Forest Trail
Description: An eco-tourism site where visitors can learn jungle survival skills from indigenous Aetas while trekking through primary forests.

Name: El Kabayo Waterfalls
Description: Two-tiered waterfalls located near a horse stable, offering an accessible and family-friendly nature escape within the Freeport Zone.

Name: Mt. Labney
Description: A remote peak on the border of Tarlac and Zambales, known for its dense forests and being part of the "Tarlac-Zambales Traverse."

Name: Anzap Twin Falls
Description: The twin falls stand around 200 feet. Water flows from the Matiya and Piccan river. It is popular to the hikers and tourists since it offers a unique view of flora and fauna, its landscape and forest trail.

Name: Forest Green Land
Description: A view of the greenlands in San Jose Tarlac with sea of clouds.

Name: Eco Park View Deck
Description: A place where you can meditate to relieve stress, bird watch, and overlook the countryside. Can also do picnic in this area.

Name: Mt. Quadrante
Description: A challenging peak in the Cawag range that offers a unique perspective of the Subic Bay mountain silhouettes.

Name: San Salvador Island
Description: An island barangay known for its award-winning marine sanctuary, giant clam nursery, and lush mangrove forests.

Name: Tukal-Tukal Falls
Description: A multi-tiered waterfall system hidden in the mountains of Sta. Cruz, featuring several cold natural pools.

Name: Linasin Falls
Description: A scenic waterfall tucked away in the rural hills of San Marcelino, popular among local hikers and bikers.

Name: Mt. Pinatubo Delta 5 Trail
Description: An extreme 4x4 and hiking route that follows the riverbeds and volcanic canyons towards the foot of Mt. Pinatubo.

Name: Cawag River
Description: A pristine river with crystal clear water and large boulders, serving as the primary water source and jump-off for the Cawag peaks.

Name: Sagrada Familia Cave
Description: A limestone cave system featuring impressive stalactite formations and a religious shrine inside, visited by pilgrims and spelunkers.

Name: Tomotogol Falls
Description: A multi-level waterfall in Masinloc with a wide swimming area, popular for family weekend outings.

Name: Desenuebe River Camp
Description: A river camp spot with a flowy stream undiscovered by outsiders

Name: Balon-Balon Falls
Description: A hidden waterfall in the forests of Palauig with a circular basin, often visited alongside the Mt. Tapulao trek.

Name: Mt. Maubanban
Description: A mountain offering a panoramic view of Mapanuepe Lake and the lahar-filled plains of Southern Zambales.

Name: Pamatawan River
Description: A scenic river used for traditional bamboo rafting and a popular spot for viewing the local Zambales mountain ranges.

Name: Mt. Redondo
Description: A peak known for its red soil and rocky terrain, providing a challenging training ground for trail runners.

Name: Mt. Agudo
Description: A sharp, pointed peak in Castillejos that offers a steep climb and a 360-degree view of the Central Luzon plains.

Name: Mt. Pink
Description: A hill near Mapanuepe Lake that glows with a pinkish hue during sunset due to the minerals in its soil.

Name: Bibig Impiyerno Falls
Description: A picturesque waterfall hidden in a narrow gorge, named for its "beautiful" (marikit) and graceful water flow.

Name: Dome's Peak Campsite
Description: A secret camping spot in Botolan with eccentric trees and vines.

Name: Camp Kainomayan
Description: A camping spot with a lot of activities such as tent pitching with a mesmerizing river.

Name: Sagpat Falls
Description: A small but powerful waterfall that cascades into a natural pool.

Name: Mt. Masinloc
Description: A mountain peak overlooking the Masinloc Power Plant and the West Philippine Sea, popular for its sunrise views.

Name: Agnain Cove
Description: This remote locale features a tiny, scenic cove & beach against a lush forest backdrop.

Name: JEST Camp
Description: The Jungle Environment Survival Training Camp, famous for being the training ground of the US Navy SEALs in the Philippines.

Name: Uacon Lake
Description: A peaceful lake in Candelaria used for fishing and boat tours, serving as the gateway to Potipot Island.

Name: Mount Tabeyo
Description: The second highest peak in Luzon, known for its pristine mossy forest and the four mystical lakes at its base.

Name: Mt. Timbak
Description: The third highest peak in Luzon, famous for its proximity to the Halsema Highway and the ancient fire mummies in nearby caves.

Name: Mt. Kabunian
Description: A sacred mountain in Bakun featuring massive rocky walls and a challenging trail leading to a summit with spiritual significance.

Name: Mt. Lobo
Description: Part of the Bakun Trio, this peak offers a trail through pine forests and rewards hikers with views of the Bakun valley.

Name: Mt. Tenglawan
Description: The highest of the Bakun Trio, known for the "Carrot" rock formation and a 360-degree view of the Ilocos and Cordillera ranges.

Name: Mt. Gedgedayan
Description: A lesser-known peak in Bakun offering a serene trekking experience through vegetable terraces and pine-clad ridges.

Name: Mt. Tagpaya
Description: A majestic peak in the Kibungan Circuit, featuring steep rocky faces and a summit that looks out over the "Switzerland of Benguet."

Name: Mt. Atok
Description: One of the highest points along the Atok Poblacion Highway, offering easy access to views of vegetable gardens and cold mountain air.

Name: Mt. Osdung
Description: One of the highest points along the Halsema Highway, offering easy access to views of vegetable gardens and cold mountain air.

Name: Mt. Purgatory
Description: Named by Spanish missionaries for its cold and misty environment, this peak is the highlight of a multi-day mossy forest trek.

Name: Mt. Pack
Description: A key peak in the Purgatory-Mangisi traverse, featuring mossy forests and serving as a gateway to the remote Benguet highlands.

Name: Mt. Komkompol
Description: The highest point of the Purgatory traverse, offering a clear view of Mt. Pulag and the surrounding Agno River valley.

Name: Dumliing Falls
Description: It is the highest waterfalls in Nueva Vizcaya called as Dumli-ing located in Brgy. Balete, Kayapa, Nueva Vizcaya and is among the top ten highest falls in the Philippines with an approximate elevation of 98 meters.

Name: Mount Mangagew
Description: A remote peak known for its dense mossy forest and as the first of six mountains in the Mt Purgatory traverse.

Name: Mt. Tangbaw
Description: A scenic campsite and peak in the Purgatory range, often used by hikers to rest before the final summit push.

Name: Mt. Shung
Description: A minor peak near Baguio City offering a quick escape into nature with views of the West Philippine Sea and the city skyline.

Name: Mt. Pigingan
Description: Known for its sharp, jagged ridge and views of the Philex mines and San Roque Dam, offering a moderately challenging day hike.

Name: Mt. Bidawan
Description: A lesser-traveled peak in Itogon featuring grasslands and pine forests, perfect for those seeking solitude in the mountains.

Name: Mt. Sto. Tomas
Description: A prominent mountain in Tuba hosting radar stations and offering panoramic views of Baguio and the Lingayen Gulf.

Name: Mt. Cabuyao
Description: Adjacent to Mt. Sto. Tomas, famous for its "Mickey Mouse" ears (radar dishes) and a viewpoint of the city and the sea.

Name: Mt. Ampacao
Description: The highest peak in Sagada, offering views of the rice terraces and the nearby towns of Besao and Tadian.

Name: Mt. Sisipitan
Description: A mountain known for its mossy forests and as a source of several rivers, offering a cool and misty trekking experience.

Name: Mt. Data
Description: A plateau and national park known for its historical significance and diverse flora, serving as a watershed for Northern Luzon. National park mountain summit rising 7,580 ft., with rich habitat for rare mammals & birdlife.

Name: Mt. Mogao
Description: A scenic peak in Tadian offering a glimpse into the traditional lifestyle of the local community and views of the Chico River.

Name: Mt. Polis
Description: A mountain pass and peak between Ifugao and Mt. Province, often shrouded in fog and offering cold temperatures year-round.

Name: Mt. Mating-oy
Description: Also known as the "Sleeping Beauty," it is a mountain range whose silhouette resembles a reclining woman, iconic to Kalinga.

Name: DapDap Falls
Description: Only about an hour’s drive from Tabuk City, the place features scenic views, the towering Tawang Falls, and the clear and cool waters of the Pangol River, making it a great spot for swimming and picnics.

Name: Masinglit Falls
Description: Falls in Tanudan with very clean and cold water.

Name: Matabor Falls
Description: Falls near Lubo Rice Terraces with heavy stream of water flowing.

Name: Lubo Rice Terraces
Description: A place if you want oneness with nature and forget the noise and worries of the world while immersing with the locals' culture.

Name: Mt. Napulauan
Description: A majestic mountain in Ifugao famous for its thick mossy forest and the legendary "sea of clouds" at its summit.

Name: Mt. Kapiligan
Description: A peak located near the Mt. Pulag National Park, offering views of the Ambangeg trail and the surrounding mountain ranges.

Name: Mount Binhagan
Description: A limestone mountain featuring caves and a summit that looks out over the capital town of Ifugao.

Name: Mt. Costa
Description: A stroll-through garden featuring over 20 different themed gardens, inspired by international landscape designs.

Name: Lumiang Cave
Description: A burial cave containing ancient coffins stacked at its entrance, serving as a profound testament to Sagada's heritage.

Name: Balangagan Cave
Description: A spiritual and historic cave used as a burial site, now open for guided tours to see its well-preserved formations and coffins.

Name: Obwa Falls
Description: Crystal-clear water, perfect spot to cool down and reconnect with nature.  A hidden gem for those who love a mix of hiking and stunning natural beauty.

Name: Bokong Falls
Description: A small but beautiful waterfall near the town center, featuring a deep natural pool perfect for a quick and refreshing swim.

Name: Buscalan Village
Description: The world-famous home of the last traditional Kalinga tattoo artists, offering a deep dive into the "mambabatok" culture.

Name: Tulgao Rice Terraces
Description: Stunning rice terraces in Tinglayan known for their semi-circular shape and the nearby Palan-ah hot springs.

Name: Bugnay Village
Description: A historic village known as the home of Macli-ing Dulag, a hero who fought against the Chico River Dam project.

Name: Luplupa Village
Description: A peaceful riverside village in Tinglayan, serving as a base for visitors exploring the rich culture and mountains of Kalinga.

Name: Ducligan Rice Terraces
Description: Unique terraces featuring a hot spring and a massive rock "wall" that frames the agricultural landscape in Banaue.

Name: Hungduan Rice Terraces
Description: A spider-web-like cluster of stone-walled terraces that are part of the UNESCO World Heritage list.

Name: Hiwang Village
Description: Beautiful views at the top of Hiwang viewpoint, great ancient wooden artifacts found in one of the native houses that contain rice Gods that were like crafted several hundred years ago

Name: Talubin Falls
Description: Hidden falls after the beautiful scenery of mountains and Rice Terraces.

Name: Lake Tufub
Description: A lake with fresh and cool mountain breeze at the top. A place to be one with nature.

Name: Mt. Amuyao
Description: An 8,865-foot mountain carpeted in lush rainforest & rice terraces & hiking route.

Name: Karanag Falls
Description: A  waterfall situated in a deep valley in Barlig with a deep pool formed at the base of the falls.

Name: Philippine Eagle's Statue
Description: A picturesque detailed mighty eagle statue that looks mystical during a sunny weather.

Name: Mt. Daraitan
Description: Popular hiking destination known for its limestone formations and clear Agos River.

Name: Mt. Binacayan
Description: Features jagged limestone and scenic views of the Sierra Madre and Wawa River.

Name: Mt. Pamitinan
Description: Known for its historical cave and rock climbing opportunities.

Name: Mt. Hapunang Banoi
Description: Highest peak in the Rodriguez trilogy, named after 'Eagle's feeding ground'.

Name: Hinulugang Taktak
Description: Iconic waterfall in Antipolo, now a restored National Park and ecotourism site.

Name: Pinto Art Museum
Description: Contemporary art gallery with Mediterranean-style buildings and lush gardens.

Name: Angono Petroglyphs
Description: Humans & animals dating from the Neolithic period are carved into the walls of this rock shelter.

Name: Cloud 9 Antipolo
Description: Famous for its 360-degree view of Metro Manila and a hanging bridge.

Name: Daranak Falls
Description: A stunning 14-meter waterfall surrounded by lush vegetation and natural pools.

Name: Batlag Falls
Description: Located just above Daranak Falls, offering a more serene and secluded atmosphere.

Name: Treasure Mountain
Description: Known for its easy-to-reach sea of clouds and rock formations.

Name: Mt. Kulis
Description: Features several photo spots like the 'Spider Web' and Noah's Ark.

Name: Pililla Wind Farm
Description: Features 27 giant wind turbines overlooking Laguna de Bay.

Name: Calinawan Cave
Description: A multi-level cave used by revolutionaries during the Spanish and Japanese periods.

Name: Wawa Dam
Description: A gravity dam situated at the foot of the Sierra Madre mountains.

Name: Regina RICA
Description: Features a massive $71$-foot statue of the Virgin Mary and ecological trails.

Name: Mystical Cave
Description: Known for its stalactite and stalagmite formations resembling religious images.

Name: Mt. Makiling
Description: A dormant volcano known for its diverse flora and fauna and legendary Maria Makiling.

Name: Mt. Banahaw
Description: A sacred mountain frequented by pilgrims and hikers

Name: Hulugan Falls
Description: A majestic 70-meter waterfall with a wide basin and dramatic cascades.

Name: Aliw Falls
Description: A multi-tiered waterfall known for its wide, curtain-like flow.

Name: Talay Falls
Description: A hidden waterfall located upstream from Hulugan Falls.

Name: Taytay Falls
Description: Also known as Majayjay Falls, known for its freezing cold, crystal-clear mountain water.

Name: Nagcarlan Underground Cemetery
Description: The only underground cemetery in the Philippines, used as a hideout during the revolution.

Name: Rizal Shrine
Description: A replica of the original house where the national hero Jose Rizal was born.

Name: Bato Springs
Description: A refreshing resort featuring natural springs and cold water pools.

Name: Cavinti Underground River and Caves
Description: Offers a unique spelunking experience through underground waterways.

Name: Japanese Garden
Description: A memorial park built to honor Japanese soldiers who died during WWII.

Name: Seven Lakes of San Pablo
Description: A group of crater lakes formed by volcanic activity, perfect for relaxation.

Name: Pandin Lake
Description: The most pristine of the seven lakes, known for its bamboo raft tours.

Name: Mount Romelo
Description: Famous for being a gateway to several stunning waterfalls like Buruwisan.

Name: Buruwisan Falls
Description: A popular campsite and waterfall located within the Sierra Madre range.

Name: Dampalit Falls
Description: A refreshing waterfall near UPLB, perfect for a quick dip.

Name: Hidden Valley Springs
Description: A secluded tropical forest resort with natural volcanic spring pools.

Name: Mt. Banahaw
Description: The main entry point for pilgrims visiting the sacred mountain.

Name: Kamay ni Hesus
Description: A popular pilgrimage site featuring a huge statue of Christ and a healing chapel.

Name: Cagbalete Island
Description: Famous for its wide sandbars and turquoise waters during low tide.

Name: Jomalig Island
Description: Remote island known for its golden sand and 'Lingayen' beach.

Name: Borawan Beach
Description: Named after its resemblance to Boracay and Palawan, with white sand and limestone cliffs.

Name: Dampalitan Beach
Description: A quiet beach near Borawan, known for its relaxing atmosphere and pine trees.

Name: Puting Buhangin
Description: Features a small cave and a pristine white sand beach.

Name: Malagunlong Bridge
Description: One of the oldest Spanish-era stone bridges in the province.

Name: Real Surf
Description: A popular weekend destination for surfers near Metro Manila.

Name: Balagbag Falls
Description: A multi-level waterfall with clear mountain water and wide pools.

Name: Nonok Falls
Description: A high waterfall located near Balagbag Falls.

Name: Mount Cristobal
Description: Often called the 'Devil's Mountain' as the eerie counterpart to Mt. Banahaw.

Name: Sariaya Beach
Description: A beach with a beautiful scenery with clean and crystal clear water.

Name: Alibijaban Island
Description: A remote island with lush mangroves and white sand beaches.

Name: Kwebang Lampas
Description: A popular destination featuring a cave that leads directly to the beach.

Name: Mount Camagon
Description: Offers a scenic view of the coastal town of Quezon on Alabat Island.

Name: Ditumabo Mother Falls
Description: A massive 140-foot waterfall with a wide, cold basin, serving as the main water source for the town of Baler.

Name: Diguisit Falls
Description: A series of small cascades located along the rocky coast of Baler, offering a refreshing freshwater stop near the ocean.

Name: Ermita Hill
Description: A historical hill that served as a refuge during the 1735 "Tromba Marina" (tidal wave) that wiped out the town of Baler.

Name: Museo de Baler
Description: A museum housing artifacts related to the Siege of Baler, ethnic groups of Aurora, and the history of the Quezon family.

Name: Dona Aurora Quezon House
Description: A replica of the birthplace of the former First Lady Aurora Quezon, showcasing traditional architecture and family memorabilia.

Name: Millennium Tree
Description: A 600-year-old balete tree so large that people can walk through its roots; it is considered one of the largest in Asia.

Name: Ditumabo Mother Falls
Description: Popular waterfall with a cold-water pool at its base, reachable by a lush 45-minute nature trek.

Name: Zabali Peak
Description: A scenic mountain trail in Baler offering a panoramic view of the Baler Bay and the surrounding Pacific coastline.

Name: Mt. Udok
Description: A challenging peak for seasoned hikers, featuring dense rainforests and serving as a vital watershed for the province.

Name: Ampere Beach
Description: Known for its smooth, dark stones and the dramatic "Tromba Marina" waves that crash against its rocky shores.

Name: Dinadiawan Beach
Description: A pristine stretch of white sand beach with a lush mountain backdrop, offering a tranquil and less commercialized atmosphere.

Name: Bulawan Falls
Description: A remote and majestic waterfall with multiple tiers, tucked deep within the virgin forests of Northern Aurora.

Name: Casapsapan Beach
Description: A hidden beach paradise with powdery sand and calm waters, perfect for swimming and camping away from the crowds.

Name: Dalugan Bay
Description: A protected bay area known for its rich marine biodiversity and scenic views of the Sierra Madre mountain range.

Name: Canawer Beach
Description: The northernmost beach in Aurora, offering vast stretches of untouched white sand and clear, unpolluted waters.

Name: Singep Falls
Description: A hidden waterfall in the highlands of Dilasag, known for its cold mountain water and peaceful forest setting.

Name: Canili-Diayo Dam
Description: A massive dam complex bordering Nueva Ecija and Aurora, offering scenic views and regulating water for the region's farms.

Name: Dicasalarin Cove
Description: Privately owned beach in a quiet bay framed by rugged headland, with walks to a nearby lighthouse.

Name: Diguisit Beach
Description: Popular for picture-taking, this picturesque coral beach features pebbly sand & unique boulders.

Name: Ubot Falls
Description: One of the most beautiful waterfalls in the Philippines. The water comes from a hole in the ceiling of the cave and crashes down about 40-50 feet. The pool is thigh deep so it's perfect for a shockingly cold dip.

Name: Baul Falls
Description: A smaller, secluded waterfall frequented by locals, offering a quiet and natural swimming experience.

Name: Sabang Beach Surf Point
Description: The primary surfing destination in Baler, known for its consistent waves and numerous surfing schools.

Name: Cemento Beach
Description: A rocky beach known for the "Cobra Reef," which produces high-quality waves for professional surfers.

Name: Mt. Pinagbanderahan
Description: A historic peak within the Quezon National Forest Park where the Philippine flag was raised during World War II.

Name: Bantakay Falls
Description: A secluded waterfall tucked inside a cave-like rock formation, accessible via a scenic trek through the forest.

Name: Quezon National Forest Park
Description: A 983-hectare protected area known for its "Bitukang Manok" zigzag road and diverse tropical flora.

Name: Tikub Lake
Description: A nearly circular crater lake surrounded by lush vegetation, offering a tranquil environment for fishing and bird watching.

Name: Kutang San Diego
Description: A Spanish-era stone fortress built in the late 18th century to defend the town against pirate raids.

Name: Pulong Pasig
Description: A pristine island featuring a long sandbar and clear waters, located within the expansive Calauag Bay.

Name: Minasawa Island
Description: A small island sanctuary known for its migratory bird population and beautiful coral reefs.

Name: Tipuan Falls
Description: Tipuan Falls is one of the best destinations in the province of Quezon. Tipuan Falls boasts its postcard-caliber beauty, clear, and cold water, as well as the lush greenery mountain.

Name: Kakawayan Falls
Description: A hidden gem in the forests of Infanta, offering a secluded and natural swimming basin for hikers.

Name: Malicboy Falls
Description: A refreshing waterfall located near the Old Zigzag Road, popular as a pitstop for travelers.

Name: Hagdan-hagdan Falls
Description: A unique staircase-like waterfall system located within the protected forest area of Atimonan.

Name: Villa Sariaya Heritage House
Description: A well-preserved ancestral mansion showcasing early 20th-century Filipino-Spanish architecture and lifestyle.

Name: Gala-Rodriguez Ancestral House
Description: An iconic Art Deco-style mansion in Sariaya, famous for its historical significance and elegant interiors.

Name: Natalio Enriquez House
Description: A heritage house designed by Andres Luna de San Pedro, known for its classic European-inspired architecture.

Name: Calauag Bay
Description: A vast bay known for its calm waters, fishing communities, and scenic boat tours to nearby islets.

Name: Guinayangan Mangrove Park
Description: A conservation area featuring extensive mangrove forests and a boardwalk for eco-tourism and bird watching.

Name: Kinabuhayan Village
Description: A spiritual village at the foot of Mt. Banahaw, known for its natural springs believed to have healing properties.

Name: Kinabuhayan Cave
Description: A quick trekking trail near the national road on which you can visit the Kinabuhayan Campsite and Yapak ni Hesus.

Name: Mt. Malepunyo
Description: A mountain range shared with Laguna and Batangas, offering lush trails and views of the Southern Luzon plains.

Name: Polillo Island Bird Sanctuary
Description: A protected area on Polillo Island dedicated to the conservation of endemic bird species and their tropical habitats.

Name: San Luis Obispo Parish Church - IV, Baler, Aurora (Prelature of Infanta)
Description: Also known as San Luis Obispo de Tolosa Parish, it is the historic site of the "Siege of Baler" (1898-1899), where the last Spanish garrison refused to surrender for 337 days.

Name: Lt. Gilmore Marker
Description: A historical marker commemorating the rescue attempt by US Navy Lieutenant James Gilmore and his men during the Philippine-American War in 1899.

Name: Quezon Memorial Park
Description: A town plaza and park dedicated to the memory of President Manuel L. Quezon, serving as the central hub for local festivities and cultural gatherings.

Name: Baler 400 Years Monument
Description: A commemorative monument built to celebrate the 400th anniversary of the town's founding, symbolizing the resilience and history of the people of Baler.

Name: Maria Aurora Cultural Center
Description: A community hub that showcases the traditional crafts, dances, and agricultural heritage of the only inland municipality in Aurora.

Name: Egongot Cultural Community
Description: An ancestral settlement of the Egongot (Ilongot) people, offering a glimpse into the indigenous customs, traditions, and pre-colonial history of the province.

Name: Dibutunan Twin Cave
